project_reference.core_research_question: What is the Hubble constant H0 as measured by dark siren inference from LISA EMRI detections?
project_reference.current_focus: v2.2 Phase 43 — Posterior Calibration Fix (SC-3 MAP=0.860 root cause diagnosis)

project_contract.scope: Diagnose and fix SC-3 MAP=0.860 failure. Two hypotheses: H1 = D(h) denominator missing from --combine/extract_baseline; H2 = CRBs store equatorial sky angles but v2.2 catalog expects ecliptic. Fix confirmed root causes. Re-run --evaluate to verify MAP ≈ 0.73 ± 0.01.
claims: FIX-01: Root cause of SC-3 MAP=0.860 identified (H1, H2, or both)
FIX-02: Root cause(s) fixed in code
deliverables: Angle audit: code-path trace from simulation to CRB CSV confirming qS/phiS frame
Bug fix commits (with [PHYSICS] prefix if formula change)
acceptance_tests: uv run python -m master_thesis_code simulations/ --evaluate shows MAP ≈ 0.73 ± 0.01
forbidden_proxies: extract_baseline MAP alone is NOT sufficient (it lacks D(h) — use --evaluate log MAP)
VERIFY-02-style comparison (pre/post same format) does not catch this bug
uncertainty_markers: If H2 (CRB frame mismatch) is confirmed, a new simulation run may be required — this would significantly expand scope
VERIFY-04 anisotropy may or may not be caused by the same bug

position.last_activity_desc: Phase 40 GAPS_FOUND; Phase 43 fix phase inserted per Q1 decision; planning in progress.

intermediate_results: Phase 40 VERIFY-03 SC-3: MAP=0.860 (expected 0.73±0.01) — extract_baseline sums log-L without D(h)
Phase 40 VERIFY-02: MAP shift 0.0% (abort gate NOT fired)
Phase 40 VERIFY-05: mean_lb=0.041 (borderline; Phase 41 SKIPPED per user Q2)
v2.2 CRB re-evaluation: 60 events at SNR≥20 (vs v2.1 417 events)
v2.2 MAP=0.73 confirmed by --evaluate comparison in VERIFY-02
open_questions: Do CRBs in simulations/prepared_cramer_rao_bounds.csv store qS/phiS in equatorial or ecliptic frame? (FIX-02)
If CRBs are equatorial (pre-COORD): does a new simulation run need to be triggered? (scope-expander)
Will VERIFY-04 anisotropy resolve after D(h)/frame fix, or is Phase 42 still needed?

decisions.summary: Q1: Insert fix phase for VERIFY-03 SC-3 (angle audit + D(h) in --combine)
decisions.rationale: MAP=0.860 from extract_baseline; root cause: D(h) missing OR CRB frame mismatch post-COORD fix
VERIFY-05 mean_lb=0.041 accepted as known limitation; 19 off-grid events (3.5%) documented

decisions.summary: Q3: Defer Phase 42 until Phase 43 Q1 resolved
decisions.rationale: Anisotropy may be a symptom of same D(h)/COORD bug, not genuine sky-dependent P_det

custom_conventions.sky_angles: qS = ecliptic colatitude (polar angle from north ecliptic pole), phiS = ecliptic longitude; GLADE equatorial RA/Dec rotated to ecliptic via astropy BarycentricTrueEcliptic (Phase 36 fix)
custom_conventions.posterior_normalization: p(h|data) ∝ sum_i log[L_i(h)] - N*log[D(h)], where D(h) is the completeness-corrected detectable volume
